conversations.content: Today we studied the Mingde Middle School Dormitory Management Code. If a student shouts loudly after lights out for the first violation...
Shouting after lights out disturbs others' rest. What's the penalty for the first time?
then the dorm leader records their name and penalizes them with cleaning the common area for one week.
Record name plus one week of cleaning — reasonable! What about unauthorized wiring or banned electrical appliances?
If a student uses unauthorized wiring or banned electrical appliances, then confiscate the appliance, issue a public criticism, and deduct 5 dorm points.
Confiscate plus public criticism and point deduction — safety first, makes sense! What about hosting outsiders or staying out overnight?
If a student hosts a non-resident overnight or stays out overnight, then issue a demerit and notify the parents to come to school to sign a safety commitment letter.
Hosting outsiders or staying out — demerit plus parent commitment letter, parents share the responsibility! What's the most severe case?
If a student smokes, drinks alcohol, or fights in the dormitory, then place them on probation and revoke their dormitory residency.
Smoking, drinking, fighting — probation plus kicked out of the dorm, that's no joke!

think: Mingde Middle School dorm management: first-time loud shouting after lights out → record name + clean common area 1 week; unauthorized wiring/banned appliances → confiscate + public criticism + deduct 5 dorm points; host outsider/overnight absence → demerit + parent signs safety commitment; smoking/drinking/fighting → probation + revoke dorm residency.
memory: Mingde Middle School Dormitory Management Code: If a student shouts loudly after lights out for the first violation, then the dorm leader records their name and penalizes them with cleaning the common area for one week; if a student uses unauthorized wiring or banned electrical appliances, then confiscate the appliance, issue a public criticism, and deduct 5 dorm points; if a student hosts a non-resident overnight or stays out overnight, then issue a demerit and notify the parents to come to school to sign a safety commitment letter; if a student smokes, drinks alcohol, or fights in the dormitory, then place them on probation and revoke their dormitory residency.
